design_decisions.choice: Function format: Follow existing Captain function structure (trigger description, inputs-loaded block, stepwise procedure, output specification, session-end commit discipline).
options: New function format unique to resolve-dirty-worktree
design_decisions.rationale: Consistency; implementers reading captain.md already navigate this format.

design_decisions.choice: Discard threshold: Conservative commit-by-default with narrow discard criteria — commit all, discard only on stray build artefacts, accidental mass-deletion outside touchpoints, or edits to files outside declared touchpoints with no coherent intent.
options: Conservative commit-by-default with narrow discard criteria
Page Coach on every dirty worktree
design_decisions.rationale: Spec Risk section explicitly prefers committing — the Verifier still runs and will FAIL bad code, whereas discarding good work is irreversible. Memory-cited: [[project_coach_loop_worktree_hygiene]] records T3/S06a and T8/S24 dirty-tree incidents as motivation.

design_decisions.choice: Detector contract: git status --porcelain, filtered to tracked changes or untracked files within the slice's declared touchpoints; non-empty → dispatch Captain's resolve-dirty-worktree.
options: Unfiltered git status --porcelain (fires on any dirty byte)
Filtered to tracked changes + touchpoint-scoped untracked files (aligns with spec Risk 2)
Filtered to tracked changes only (misses new files)
design_decisions.rationale: Spec Risk 2 requires scoping the dirty-check to tracked changes + intended touchpoints. Filtering at the detector level (rather than classifying in the Captain) prevents false-positive dispatches on a stray sworn binary or node_modules drift.

design_decisions.choice: Journal record format: Prescriptive structured format — impacted files (git status --porcelain output), one-line diff characterisation, decision (committed/discarded/escalated), rationale.
design_decisions.rationale: Consistency and audit trail. A prescriptive format ensures every resolution carries the same evidence and is mechanically inspectable.
